02 Types of Value Engines - YouTube.mp4
The value creation process can vary wildly across different businesses and industries, but in my experience, all value engines fall into three core categories. There are growth engines, fulfillment engines, and innovation engines. Growth engines visualize the process by which new customers are acquired and sold. Fulfillment engines visualize the process by which those customers receive the promised value after the sale is actually made. And innovation engines visualize the process by which products and service offerings are created, updated, and improved. Now, as a side note, we aren't going to be focusing as much on innovation engines in this program, simply because they just vary wildly from company to company, so they aren't as universally applicable as growth and fulfillment engines. That said, the process of mapping an innovation engine is the same process that you're going to go through to map your other value engines.
Once you have mapped your core growth and fulfillment engines, feel free to go back and tackle your innovation engines. Also, I recognize that there are other important value creation processes in a company that relate to operational and administrative functions. We're going to cover those later, but since growth engines and fulfillment engines are both customer-facing, they are going to be the focus of this session. All right, so with all that said, let's take a deeper dive into the two types of value engines that we're going to be discussing in this program, starting with the growth engine.
